name: Mortgage Lifter
image_alt: A large smooth pink-red beefsteak tomato
description: A massive, meaty pink beefsteak with very few seeds and thick flesh. One of the most reliably productive large heirlooms, with a sweet, low-acid flavor that makes it universally appealing.
history: Developed in the 1930s by M.C. 'Radiator Charlie' Byles of Logan, West Virginia. Over six years he crossed the four largest tomatoes he could find. He sold plants for $1 each and paid off his $6,000 mortgage in six years — hence the name.

good.reason: Classic companion — repels pests, may enhance flavor

bad.reason: Allelopathic — inhibits growth of most garden plants
Compete for nutrients and attract conflicting pests

seasonal_quirks: Very forgiving across a wide range of conditions. Fruits crack at the shoulders in heavy rain — consistent watering reduces this. Sets fruit reliably even in heat.
harvest_cues: Deep pink-red color across the entire fruit with slight give under pressure. Fruits can reach 2 lbs — support heavy clusters to prevent vine damage.
variety_notes: Often compared to Brandywine but more productive and beginner-friendly. Low acid makes it good for those sensitive to acidic tomatoes. Meaty flesh with small seed cavities is excellent for slicing.
preservation: Excellent for sauce and canning due to meaty flesh and low water content. Freezes well.
